questions.question_text: Answer the following questions CO Mapping a Define a difference equation with an example. 2.5 b What is a lag operator and how is it used in time series modeling? 2.5 c What conditions make an ARMA model stationary? 2.5 d Differentiate between autocorrelation and partial autocorrelation functions. 2.5 e What are the stylized facts of economic time series? 2.5 f Write a brief note on the GARCH model. 2.5 g Explain the concept of a stochastic trend. 2.5 h What is the purpose of the Dickey-Fuller test in time series analysis? 2.5
Explain the cobweb model and illustrate its solution using both graphical and algebraic methods. 10
Using the method of undetermined coefficients, solve the non- homogeneous difference equation: yt−0.8yt−1=5. Interpret the solution in the context of a time series application. 10
Discuss the Box-Jenkins methodology in detail. How do Auto Correlation Function and Partial Auto Correlation Function help in model identification? 10
Define stationary in the context of time series. Discuss why stationary is essential for ARMA model estimation, with the help of an example. 10
Discuss the concept of volatility clustering in financial time series. How do ARCH models capture this phenomenon? 10
Develop a GARCH model to estimate inflation volatility. Explain the estimation procedure and interpretation of the model parameters. 10
Perform a Dickey-Fuller test on a time series with an upward trend. Explain each step of the testing procedure and how you would interpret the results. 10
What is a unit root in time series data? How can its presence affect model selection and forecasting? 10
